Name: spectral_inference_optimization
Title: Spectral Inference Optimization: A Training-Free Approach for Latency-Aware Machine Learning Models
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that a training-free optimization framework utilizing spectral analysis of activation covariances can significantly enhance inference efficiency across various ML models, minimizing latency and computational overhead.
Related Work: While recent studies have explored dynamic precision allocation and geometric signal separation, they often rely on training or specific architectures. Our approach distinctly employs zero-training spectral analysis to optimize inference, focusing on a generalizable framework that can be applied broadly.
Abstract: In the pursuit of efficient machine learning inference, we propose a novel framework, Spectral Inference Optimization (SIO), which leverages real-time spectral analysis of activation covariances to optimize inference without the need for training. By identifying and projecting onto low-rank subspaces of dominant spectral directions, SIO can dynamically adjust the computational load while strictly adhering to latency constraints. This method not only reduces hidden-state perturbation overhead but also provides a transparent mechanism for optimizing model performance under varying input conditions. We will validate SIO across diverse ML architectures, measuring its impact on latency, throughput, and accuracy. Our approach promises to bridge foundational optimization principles with practical applications in system-scale inference, offering a reproducible pathway for efficient model deployment in real-world scenarios.
Experiments: Evaluate the latency and accuracy of SIO across standard ML benchmarks using various architectures (e.g., CNNs, RNNs, Transformers).
Implement a controlled experiment comparing SIO with existing training-based optimization methods to highlight its advantages in terms of runtime efficiency and computational overhead.
Measure the impact of varying input distributions on inference performance to assess the robustness of SIO under different operational conditions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the reliance on accurate spectral analysis, which may be sensitive to noise in activation patterns. Limitations may involve the generalizability of the proposed approach across all model types, as certain architectures may not benefit as significantly from spectral optimization.
